headline: Full 15-game Friday slate — no confirmed under edges
body: Second half is in full swing with 15 games (30 starters; Mets and Phillies off). We scanned every starter for a confirmed short-outing reason — an announced pitch/inning cap, a planned opener/bullpen game, or a live injury build-up. Nothing qualified. The names that look tempting are already priced: Roki Sasaki, Jared Jones, Shane McClanahan and Jeffrey Springs are all past their build-ups (Sasaki went 6 IP into the break, McClanahan's on a QS streak and lined up for Sunday), Griffin Jax and Jake Bennett are established starters now, and Logan Henderson's rookie pitch ramp is public knowledge the book already bakes in. No announced caps or openers on the board, so it's a pass. Tendencies, not guarantees; verify starters at first pitch; flat small units, never chase.
